data.provenance: tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via exploration probe (dispatches=3 < 5))
tier mid (config routing.map), marginal-cost auto (via exploration probe (dispatches=3 < 5))

data.summary: Added fail-safe journal narration for run and resume without changing journal bytes or replay.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
src/cli/commands/resume.ts |  3 ++-
 src/cli/commands/run.ts    |  3 ++-
 src/run/daemon.ts          |  5 +++--
 src/run/journal.ts         | 31 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++----
 tests/run/daemon.test.ts   | 17 +++++++++++++++++
 tests/run/journal.test.ts  | 45 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++--
 6 files changed, 94 insertions(+), 10 deletions(-)

✓ Journal gains an optional narration callback invoked on every append; when unset behavior is byte-identical to today: Journal constructor/create/open take an optional `narrate` param; append() calls `this.narrate?.(row)` after the appendFileSync, so unset callers take the identical write path. Test proves silent vs narrated journals produce byte-identical journal.jsonl files.
✓ tickmarkr run and tickmarkr resume enable narration printing one short line per event at append time: Both src/cli/commands/run.ts and resume.ts pass `narrate: (event) => console.log(formatJournalNarration(event))` to runDaemon; the formatter emits `event — taskId — detail` capped at 120 chars, and narration fires inside append(). Daemon test confirms the callback receives exactly the events appended by run and resume.
✓ journal.jsonl content unaffected; replayStatuses/replayResumeState unchanged: Narration happens after the appendFileSync and only observes the already-serialized row; journal.test.ts asserts byte-equal files and equal replayStatuses()/replayResumeState() outputs with narration on vs off.
✓ narration callback failures never crash or fail the run: append() wraps `this.narrate?.(row)` in try/catch with an empty catch; test proves a throwing callback does not make append throw and the row is still persisted (broken.read() has length 1).
✓ no new runtime dependency and no journal event-shape change: Diff touches no package.json/lockfile and imports only existing modules; JournalEvent interface and the appended row shape `{ts, event, taskId?, data}` are unchanged — formatJournalNarration is a pure additive export.

data.summary: Added 'Run output and narration' section to README documentinglive journal narration and journal.jsonl as source of truth.

data.details: ✓ README run section mentions live narration output and that journal.jsonl remains the source of truth: The diff adds a 'Run output and narration' section directly after the run command documentation stating that `tickmarkr run` streams live narration of journal events to stdout and that the append-only `.tickmarkr/runs/<runId>/journal.jsonl` ledger 'remains the authoritative source of truth' — both required elements are explicitly present in the added README text.

data.summary: Added tests/run/narration.test.ts with 3 passing regressions proving narration streams run-start/task-dispatch/run-end, is a byte-identical side-channel (mod wall-clock ts),and survives a throwing sink without dropping events.

data.details:
✓ a fake-adapter run with narration enabled emits narration lines for at least run-start, task-dispatch, and run-end: First test runs the fake adapter with a narrate callback collecting formatJournalNarration(e) lines and asserts a line starting with each of 'run-start', 'task-dispatch', and 'run-end' exists, plus stream-count parity with the on-disk journal.
✓ the journal file produced with narration enabled is byte-identical to one produced without it for the same scripted run: Second test runs the identical scripted run twice (same runId, git author/committer dates pinned so SHAs match) with and without narration and asserts the raw journal files are equal after masking only the ts field. The ts deviation is unavoidable wall-clock nondeterminism present in both runs regardless of narration (the daemon polls Date.now()), and every other byte — events, order, data, refs, SHAs — is required to be exact, which is what proves narration adds/drops/alters nothing.
✓ a narration callback that throws does not fail the run and the journal still records every event: Third test uses a narrate callback that always throws, asserts the run still completes (done=['T1'], failed=[]), and asserts the journal's full in-order event sequence equals that of a reference run with no narration — proving no event was dropped or reordered.
